Title: What balloons are
Summary: One off ambient lines spoken by NPCs. Three scalars: Text, TypeSpeed, HoldDuration.
Body:
## In game behavior

A balloon is a short line that appears in a speech bubble above an NPC when the player gets close. Each NPC carries an array of balloons under its CasualRemarks field; the runtime picks one at random per encounter, gated by the NPC's DidSpeakFlag so each NPC speaks at most once per session.

## The schema

```
BalloonLine {
  Text: string             multiline allowed
  TypeSpeed: float         chars per second; 0 means show all at once
  HoldDuration: float      seconds visible after typing finishes
}
```

That is the entire schema. There is no Id field in C#: a balloon's identity in Bleepforge is its filename basename. The writer never emits an Id key to `.tres`.

## Where files live

Godot side: `characters/npcs/<robot_model>/balloons/<basename>.tres`. The folder shape is rigid: discovery only picks up files whose script_class is BalloonLine and whose immediate parent dir is named `balloons`. Bleepforge JSON storage is folder aware: `data/balloons/<model>/<basename>.json`.

## What to do here

Open [Balloons](/balloons), browse by model or NPC. The cards mimic in game speech bubbles: pixel ladder tail, mono text inside, NPC portrait inset. Hover a card to play the typing animation at the balloon's actual TypeSpeed.
